perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that if the other party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, the damages will be irreparable and difficult to measure and that the other party shall be entitled to equitable relief including a preliminary injunction in addition to any other relief available.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that if the other party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, the damages will be irreparable and difficult to measure and that the other party shall be entitled to equitable relief including a preliminary injunction in addition to any other relief available. Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the sentence regarding damages will reduce enforceability, because it is no longer clearly tied to the enforcement of the contract. It is now inside another section.
perturbation.law_explanation: Arizona Revised Statutes § 12-1801 allows for injunctive relief for specific performance. By placing the clause acknowledging the right to equitable relief in a section detailing acceptable information usage, it weakens the explicit acknowledgement of the right to injunctive relief and potentially compromises the court's ability to readily enforce specific performance by injunction. This is because the clause now appears disconnected from enforcement provisions.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 12-1801 - Granting of injunctions; grounds 12-1801 . Granting of injunctions; grounds Judges of the superior court may grant writs of injunction: 1. When it appears that the party applying for the writ is entitled to the relief demanded, and such relief or any part thereof requires the restraint of some act prejudicial to the applicant. 2. When, pending litigation, it appears that a party is doing some act respecting the subject of litigation, or threatens or is about to do some act, or is procuring or suffering some act to be done, in violation of the rights of the applicant, which would tend to render the judgment ineffectual. 3. In all other cases when applicant is entitled to an injunction under the principles of equity.

perturbation.justification: The original text clearly links the acknowledgment of irreparable damages and the entitlement to equitable relief (including a preliminary injunction) to actions enforcing the agreement. By moving this sentence to a different section, specifically one dealing with the use of confidential information, the changed text weakens this explicit link. This creates a structural flaw where a crucial aspect of enforcement is disconnected from the enforcement provisions, potentially impacting a party's ability to readily obtain injunctive relief as outlined in Arizona Revised Statutes § 12-1801, which allows judges to grant injunctions when a party is entitled to relief that requires the restraint of some prejudicial act.

perturbation.original_text: A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement at any time shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party's right thereafter to enforce and compel compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement at any time shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party's right thereafter to enforce and compel compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the waiver clause to the beginning, in the section defining acceptable uses of confidential information, makes it less apparent and reduces its enforceability. It is no longer tied to the terms and conditions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Arizona Revised Statutes § 47-1207 governs waiver of rights. By positioning the waiver clause within a section concerning acceptable uses of confidential information, the modification causes a potential misunderstanding of its intended scope. Specifically, it may imply that the waiver only applies to breaches related to confidential information usage, and not to breaches of other contractual terms. The location misdirects and reduces the clarity of its intended application across all terms, potentially undermining the enforceability of the non-waiver provision, as it is meant to apply to 'any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms'.

Improvements of streets about the Capitol. In the improvements of streets about the Capitol, the Secretary of the Interior shall assess and collect the cost of all improvements made in front of all private property in the same proportion as charged by the District authorities for the same purpose. (R.S., D. C., Â§ 152; enacted, Apr. 9, 1997, D.C. Law 11-254, Â§ 2, 44 DCR 1575 .) Prior Codifications 1981 Ed., Â§â47-1207. 1973 Ed., Â§â47-1107.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation is classified as a Structural Flaw - Legal Contradiction, which occurs when the organization of a contract affects its clarity or enforceability, specifically when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The Law Explanation supports this by stating the placement of the waiver clause within the section concerning acceptable uses of confidential information may imply that the waiver only applies to breaches related to confidential information usage, undermining the intended application across all terms. Scraped Law Snippet 1, while not directly applicable, highlights the importance of proper legal sectioning. The change in text reduces enforceability by misdirecting the reader.
